records.ety: [L., fr. Gr. <?/; 'o`noma, 'ono`matos, a name + poiei^n to make.]

defs: The formation of words in imitation of sounds; a figure of speech in which the sound of a word is imitative of the sound of the thing which the word represents; as, the buzz of bees; the hiss of a goose; the crackle of fire.
notes: ☞ It has been maintained by some philologist that all primary words, especially names, were formed by imitation of natural sounds, but this is not believed by most.
